docs.title: PROtein — first-principles folding
docs.text:
PROtein (PyPI: protein-folder) predicts protein structure from HQIV (Horizon-centric Quantum Information and Vacuum) physics: no machine learning, no PDB-derived statistics, and no empirical molecular mechanics force fields. Numerical targets and discrete structures are tied to the formal proofs in hqiv-lean.

Government-use restriction

This software is MIT-licensed with an additional government-use restriction. See Government use & license and GOVERNMENT_USE.md before deployment.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
Image credit: Grok (from the provided picture)

  • Co-translational ribosome tunnel — null-cone search, lip plane, binary-tree segment scheduling, optional thermal steps (lean_ribosome_tunnel_pipeline.py).
  • Analytical horizon gradients — informational energy and CA+bond models (folding_energy.py).
  • Deterministic L-BFGS and hierarchical / JAX-accelerated paths where enabled (full_protein_minimizer.py).
  • HQIV-native sparse gate + pruning (optional) — OSHoracle sparse refinement expands sparse support horizon-causally, applies an HQIV-native sparse pi-phase gate evolution, then prunes to only the flipped support before the CA minimization update (osh_oracle_folding.py, pipeline_interchange.py).
  • Lean parity tests — Python scalars match Lean anchors (e.g. long-range hydrogen-bond proxy, collective modes); see tests/.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
• Temperature-aware folding / “body → cryo” schedule
  • Today the pipelines (single-chain, tunnel, assembly, ligands) are pure deterministic minimizers on \(E_\text{tot}\) (gradient descent / L‑BFGS style), without any explicit temperature parameter or annealing schedule. There is no distinction between “body temperature” folding and a final “cryo” quench; all stages are effectively \(T \to 0\) searches of the same energy landscape.
  • Roadmap: introduce a temperature-like schedule to better mimic biology + crystallography:
    • Early “folding” phase that behaves more like body temperature (e.g. stochastic perturbations, simulated annealing, or temperature-scaled clash/horizon terms).
    • Late “freeze-out” phase tuned for cryo / crystallographic conditions (stronger clash, reduced noise, tighter convergence) before final PDB export.
    • Expose knobs in the API (e.g. fold_temperature, cryo_refine=True, or a small preset enum) and document in /help and the README.

docs.title: horizon_physics.proteins.folding_energy
docs.text:
Folding energy E_tot from HQIV: minimization for peptides/proteins.

E_tot = Σ m c² + Σ ħ c / Θ_i (informational-energy) plus geometric damping f_φ = −γ′ φ / (a_loc + φ/6)² ∇φ and solvent-excluded volume (via φ). Simple rigorous minimizer: gradient descent on E_tot with Θ_i and φ from lattice positions. No force fields; all from first principles.

Returns: energy in eV (or relative units), minimized coordinates. MIT License, Python 3.10+, numpy only.

docs.title: backbone_bond_edges(n_res)
docs.text:
Covalent backbone edges for standard layout N, CA, C, O per residue (4 atoms/residue).

Returns list of (atom_i, atom_j, r_eq_angstrom) for N–CA, CA–C, C=O, and peptide C–N.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def backbone_bond_edges(n_res: int) -> List[Tuple[int, int, float]]:
    """
    Covalent backbone edges for standard layout N, CA, C, O per residue (4 atoms/residue).

    Returns list of (atom_i, atom_j, r_eq_angstrom) for N–CA, CA–C, C=O, and peptide C–N.
    """
    n = int(n_res)
    if n < 1:
        return []
    bl = backbone_bond_lengths()
    r_nca = float(bl["N_Calpha"])
    r_cac = float(bl["Calpha_C"])
    r_co = float(bl["C_O"])
    r_cn = float(bl["C_N"])
    edges: List[Tuple[int, int, float]] = []
    for i in range(n):
        b = 4 * i
        edges.append((b, b + 1, r_nca))
        edges.append((b + 1, b + 2, r_cac))
        edges.append((b + 2, b + 3, r_co))
        if i < n - 1:
            edges.append((b + 2, b + 4, r_cn))
    return edges

docs.title: bond_valley_em_scalar(r, *, r_eq=R_CA_CA_EQ, r_min=R_BOND_MIN, r_max=R_BOND_MAX, k_bond=K_BOND)
docs.text:
Scalar Cα–Cα bond penalty for one edge distance r (same piecewise form as in e_tot_ca_with_bonds).

Matches the HQIVMolecules bondValleyEM / sumValleyPotentialEM edge contribution in the Python discretization (quadratic well outside [r_min, r_max], softer well inside).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def bond_valley_em_scalar(
    r: float,
    *,
    r_eq: float = R_CA_CA_EQ,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
) -> float:
    """
    Scalar Cα–Cα bond penalty for one edge distance r (same piecewise form as in ``e_tot_ca_with_bonds``).

    Matches the HQIVMolecules ``bondValleyEM`` / ``sumValleyPotentialEM`` edge contribution in the
    Python discretization (quadratic well outside [r_min, r_max], softer well inside).
    """
    if r < 1e-12:
        r = 1.0
    if r < r_min:
        return float(k_bond * (r_min - r) ** 2)
    if r > r_max:
        return float(k_bond * (r - r_max) ** 2)
    return float(k_bond * 0.1 * (r - r_eq) ** 2)

docs.title: build_bond_poles(positions, r_eq=R_CA_CA_EQ, r_min=R_BOND_MIN, r_max=R_BOND_MAX, k_bond=K_BOND)
docs.text:
Bond potential vectors (poles) for consecutive Cα–Cα. Each pole (i, j, vec) has vec pointing i→j: force on i is −vec, on j is +vec.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def build_bond_poles(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    r_eq: float = R_CA_CA_EQ,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
) -> List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]]:
    """
    Bond potential vectors (poles) for consecutive Cα–Cα. Each pole (i, j, vec)
    has vec pointing i→j: force on i is −vec, on j is +vec.
    """
    n = positions.shape[0]
    poles: List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]] = []
    for i in range(n - 1):
        j = i + 1
        d = positions[j] - positions[i]
        r = np.linalg.norm(d)
        if r < 1e-12:
            continue
        u = d / r
        if r < r_min:
            g = -2 * k_bond * (r_min - r)
        elif r > r_max:
            g = 2 * k_bond * (r - r_max)
        else:
            g = 2 * k_bond * 0.1 * (r - r_eq)
        vec = g * u  # i→j
        poles.append(_pole(i, j, vec))
    return poles

docs.title: build_bond_poles_segments(positions, segment_ends, r_eq=R_CA_CA_EQ, r_min=R_BOND_MIN, r_max=R_BOND_MAX, k_bond=K_BOND)
docs.text:
Bond poles only within segments. segment_ends = [n1, n2, ...] gives exclusive end indices (chain 1: 0..n1-1, chain 2: n1..n2-1, ...). No bond between n1-1 and n1.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def build_bond_poles_segments(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    segment_ends: List[int],
    r_eq: float = R_CA_CA_EQ,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
) -> List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]]:
    """
    Bond poles only within segments. segment_ends = [n1, n2, ...] gives exclusive
    end indices (chain 1: 0..n1-1, chain 2: n1..n2-1, ...). No bond between n1-1 and n1.
    """
    n = positions.shape[0]
    poles: List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]] = []
    start = 0
    for end in segment_ends:
        end = min(end, n)
        for i in range(start, end - 1):
            j = i + 1
            d = positions[j] - positions[i]
            r = np.linalg.norm(d)
            if r < 1e-12:
                continue
            u = d / r
            if r < r_min:
                g = -2 * k_bond * (r_min - r)
            elif r > r_max:
                g = 2 * k_bond * (r - r_max)
            else:
                g = 2 * k_bond * 0.1 * (r - r_eq)
            poles.append(_pole(i, j, g * u))
        start = end
    return poles

docs.title: build_horizon_poles(positions, z_list, r_ref=2.0, r_horizon=R_HORIZON, k_horizon=0.5 * HBAR_C_EV_ANG, use_neighbor_list=USE_NEIGHBOR_LIST, em_scale=1.0, neighbor_cutoff=None)
docs.text:
Build bond potential vectors (poles) for horizon forces. Each pole is (i, j, vec) with vec pointing i→j: force on i is −vec, on j is +vec. Returns list of poles for all pairs within cutoff.

em_scale: Multiplier on the horizon pole strength (default 1). For aqueous solvent, use 1/ε_r to match HQIV Lean waterDielectricValley (EM term ∝ 1/(ε_r r)). neighbor_cutoff: When use_neighbor_list is True, cap spatial neighbor search at this distance (Å); default is module CUTOFF. Smaller values prune weak long pairs (faster).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def build_horizon_poles(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    r_ref: float = 2.0,
    r_horizon: float = R_HORIZON,
    k_horizon: float = 0.5 * HBAR_C_EV_ANG,
    use_neighbor_list: bool = USE_NEIGHBOR_LIST,
    em_scale: float = 1.0,
    neighbor_cutoff: Optional[float] = None,
) -> List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]]:
    """
    Build bond potential vectors (poles) for horizon forces. Each pole is (i, j, vec)
    with vec pointing i→j: force on i is −vec, on j is +vec.
    Returns list of poles for all pairs within cutoff.

    em_scale: Multiplier on the horizon pole strength (default 1). For aqueous solvent,
    use 1/ε_r to match HQIV Lean `waterDielectricValley` (EM term ∝ 1/(ε_r r)).
    neighbor_cutoff: When ``use_neighbor_list`` is True, cap spatial neighbor search at this
    distance (Å); default is module ``CUTOFF``. Smaller values prune weak long pairs (faster).
    """
    n = positions.shape[0]
    base = theta_local(6, 2)
    cap = float(neighbor_cutoff) if neighbor_cutoff is not None else CUTOFF
    cutoff = min(r_horizon, cap) if use_neighbor_list else r_horizon
    poles: List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]] = []
    if use_neighbor_list:
        neigh = build_neighbor_list(positions, cutoff=cutoff)
        for i in range(n):
            for j, r, unit in neigh[i]:
                theta_ij = base * min(1.0, r / r_ref)
                phi = horizon_scalar(theta_ij)
                pot = em_scale * k_horizon * phi / (theta_ij + 1e-9)
                vec = pot * unit  # i→j: − at i, + at j
                poles.append(_pole(i, j, vec))
    else:
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                d = positions[j] - positions[i]
                r = np.linalg.norm(d)
                if r < 1e-9 or r > r_horizon:
                    continue
                unit = d / r
                theta_ij = base * min(1.0, r / r_ref)
                phi = horizon_scalar(theta_ij)
                pot = em_scale * k_horizon * phi / (theta_ij + 1e-9)
                vec = pot * unit
                poles.append(_pole(i, j, vec))
    return poles

docs.title: build_neighbor_list(pos, cutoff=CUTOFF)
docs.text:
Pairs within cutoff; neigh[i] = [(j, r, unit), ...] for j > i only. Uses cKDTree if scipy available.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def build_neighbor_list(pos: np.ndarray, cutoff: float = CUTOFF) -> list:
    """Pairs within cutoff; neigh[i] = [(j, r, unit), ...] for j > i only. Uses cKDTree if scipy available."""
    n = len(pos)
    neigh = [[] for _ in range(n)]
    if _SCIPY_SPATIAL and n > 10:
        tree = _cKDTree(pos)
        for i in range(n):
            idx = tree.query_ball_point(pos[i], cutoff)
            for j in idx:
                if j <= i:
                    continue
                d = pos[j] - pos[i]
                r = np.linalg.norm(d)
                if r < 1e-9 or r >= cutoff:
                    continue
                neigh[i].append((j, r, d / r))
    else:
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                d = pos[j] - pos[i]
                r = np.linalg.norm(d)
                if r < 1e-9 or r >= cutoff:
                    continue
                unit = d / r
                neigh[i].append((j, r, unit))
    return neigh

docs.title: count_nonlocal_pairs_entering_horizon(pos_before, pos_after, *, r_horizon=R_HORIZON, min_seq_sep=3, eps=1e-09)
docs.text:
Count non-bonded pairs whose separation crosses into the horizon sphere.

A pair (i, j) with sequence separation >= min_seq_sep counts if it was strictly outside the horizon radius before the step (r_prev > r_horizon) and is inside or on the boundary after (r_after <= r_horizon).

This matches the onset of direct horizon (EM) coupling at R_HORIZON (default 15 Å) in build_horizon_poles / grad_horizon_full.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def count_nonlocal_pairs_entering_horizon(
    pos_before: np.ndarray,
    pos_after: np.ndarray,
    *,
    r_horizon: float = R_HORIZON,
    min_seq_sep: int = 3,
    eps: float = 1e-9,
) -> int:
    """
    Count non-bonded pairs whose separation crosses **into** the horizon sphere.

    A pair (i, j) with sequence separation >= ``min_seq_sep`` counts if it was
    strictly outside the horizon radius before the step (r_prev > r_horizon)
    and is inside or on the boundary after (r_after <= r_horizon).

    This matches the onset of direct horizon (EM) coupling at ``R_HORIZON``
    (default 15 Å) in ``build_horizon_poles`` / ``grad_horizon_full``.
    """
    a = np.asarray(pos_before, dtype=float)
    b = np.asarray(pos_after, dtype=float)
    if a.shape != b.shape or a.ndim != 2 or a.shape[1] != 3:
        raise ValueError("count_nonlocal_pairs_entering_horizon: positions must be matching (n,3).")
    n = int(a.shape[0])
    r_h = float(r_horizon)
    ms = int(min_seq_sep)
    cnt = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            if j - i < ms:
                continue
            rb = float(np.linalg.norm(a[j] - a[i]))
            ra = float(np.linalg.norm(b[j] - b[i]))
            if rb > r_h + eps and ra <= r_h + eps:
                cnt += 1
    return int(cnt)

docs.title: count_nonlocal_pairs_leaving_horizon(pos_before, pos_after, *, r_horizon=R_HORIZON, min_seq_sep=3, eps=1e-09)
docs.text:
Count non-bonded pairs whose separation crosses out of the horizon sphere.

A pair (i, j) with sequence separation >= min_seq_sep counts if it was inside or on the horizon boundary before (r_prev <= r_horizon) and is strictly outside after (r_after > r_horizon) — i.e. direct horizon (EM) coupling between those residues drops off in build_horizon_poles.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def count_nonlocal_pairs_leaving_horizon(
    pos_before: np.ndarray,
    pos_after: np.ndarray,
    *,
    r_horizon: float = R_HORIZON,
    min_seq_sep: int = 3,
    eps: float = 1e-9,
) -> int:
    """
    Count non-bonded pairs whose separation crosses **out of** the horizon sphere.

    A pair (i, j) with sequence separation >= ``min_seq_sep`` counts if it was
    inside or on the horizon boundary before (r_prev <= r_horizon) and is
    strictly outside after (r_after > r_horizon) — i.e. direct horizon (EM)
    coupling between those residues drops off in ``build_horizon_poles``.
    """
    a = np.asarray(pos_before, dtype=float)
    b = np.asarray(pos_after, dtype=float)
    if a.shape != b.shape or a.ndim != 2 or a.shape[1] != 3:
        raise ValueError("count_nonlocal_pairs_leaving_horizon: positions must be matching (n,3).")
    n = int(a.shape[0])
    r_h = float(r_horizon)
    ms = int(min_seq_sep)
    cnt = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            if j - i < ms:
                continue
            rb = float(np.linalg.norm(a[j] - a[i]))
            ra = float(np.linalg.norm(b[j] - b[i]))
            if rb <= r_h + eps and ra > r_h + eps:
                cnt += 1
    return int(cnt)

docs.title: count_variational_pairs_ca(positions, *, dist_cutoff=12.0, min_seq_sep=3, max_pairs=400)
docs.text:
Count active nonlocal Cα pairs used by the variational pair term.

This count is used for Lean-style lower-bound bookkeeping: each active pair contributes at least -epsilon.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def count_variational_pairs_ca(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    dist_cutoff: float = 12.0,
    min_seq_sep: int = 3,
    max_pairs: int = 400,
) -> int:
    """
    Count active nonlocal Cα pairs used by the variational pair term.

    This count is used for Lean-style lower-bound bookkeeping:
    each active pair contributes at least ``-epsilon``.
    """
    n = int(positions.shape[0])
    if n < 2:
        return 0
    count = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + max(1, int(min_seq_sep)), n):
            if count >= int(max_pairs):
                return count
            d = positions[j] - positions[i]
            r = float(np.linalg.norm(d))
            if r < 1e-9 or r > float(dist_cutoff):
                continue
            count += 1
    return count

docs.title: e_atomic_site(positions, z_list, i, a_loc=A_LOC_ANG)
docs.text:
Per-site contribution to e_tot: ħc/Θ_i + λ_damp * (φ_i / (a_loc + φ_i/6)).

Summing e_atomic_site(..., i) over i equals e_tot(positions, z_list) (same formulas as e_tot_informational / e_tot_damping). This is the Python analogue of an atomic / site term in assembly_foldEnergy_branch_eq (Lean Hqiv.Physics.HQIVMolecules).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_atomic_site(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    i: int,
    a_loc: float = A_LOC_ANG,
) -> float:
    """
    Per-site contribution to ``e_tot``: ħc/Θ_i + λ_damp * (φ_i / (a_loc + φ_i/6)).

    Summing ``e_atomic_site(..., i)`` over i equals ``e_tot(positions, z_list)`` (same formulas as
    ``e_tot_informational`` / ``e_tot_damping``). This is the Python analogue of an atomic / site
    term in ``assembly_foldEnergy_branch_eq`` (Lean ``Hqiv.Physics.HQIVMolecules``).
    """
    theta_i = theta_at_position(positions, i, int(z_list[i]))
    e_info = HBAR_C_EV_ANG / theta_i if theta_i > 0 else 0.0
    phi_i = horizon_scalar(theta_i)
    denom = a_loc + phi_i / 6.0
    u = phi_i / denom if denom > 0 else 0.0
    lambda_damp = 0.1 * HBAR_C_EV_ANG
    return float(e_info + lambda_damp * u)

docs.title: e_clash_penalty_ca_windowed(positions, *, r_clash=R_CLASH, k_clash=K_CLASH, window=20)
docs.text:
Clash penalty matching e_tot_ca_with_bonds (non-bonded pairs within window).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_clash_penalty_ca_windowed(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    r_clash: float = R_CLASH,
    k_clash: float = K_CLASH,
    window: int = 20,
) -> float:
    """Clash penalty matching ``e_tot_ca_with_bonds`` (non-bonded pairs within window)."""
    e = 0.0
    for i, j, r, _ in _clash_pairs(positions, r_clash, window):
        e += k_clash * (r_clash - r) ** 2
    return float(e)

docs.title: e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca(positions, *, theta_ref_rad=None, exponent=1.0)
docs.text:
Center-weighted local bend penalty on Cα triples.

A bend at the chain center carries higher effective inertia than near termini.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    theta_ref_rad: Optional[float] = None,
    exponent: float = 1.0,
) -> float:
    """
    Center-weighted local bend penalty on Cα triples.

    A bend at the chain center carries higher effective inertia than near termini.
    """
    ca = np.asarray(positions, dtype=float)
    n = ca.shape[0]
    if n < 3:
        return 0.0
    theta_ref = float(np.pi if theta_ref_rad is None else theta_ref_rad)
    w = _chain_inertia_profile(n, exponent=float(exponent))
    total = 0.0
    for i in range(1, n - 1):
        u = ca[i - 1] - ca[i]
        v = ca[i + 1] - ca[i]
        nu = float(np.linalg.norm(u))
        nv = float(np.linalg.norm(v))
        if nu < 1e-12 or nv < 1e-12:
            continue
        c = float(np.clip(np.dot(u / nu, v / nv), -1.0, 1.0))
        th = float(np.arccos(c))
        d = th - theta_ref
        total += float(w[i]) * (d * d)
    return float(total)

docs.title: e_sequential_bond_penalty_ca(positions, *, r_eq=R_CA_CA_EQ, r_min=R_BOND_MIN, r_max=R_BOND_MAX, k_bond=K_BOND)
docs.text:
Sum of bond_valley_em_scalar over consecutive Cα pairs (i, i+1).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_sequential_bond_penalty_ca(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    r_eq: float = R_CA_CA_EQ,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
) -> float:
    """Sum of ``bond_valley_em_scalar`` over consecutive Cα pairs (i, i+1)."""
    n = positions.shape[0]
    if n < 2:
        return 0.0
    d = positions[1:] - positions[:-1]
    r = np.linalg.norm(d, axis=1)
    r = np.where(r < 1e-12, 1.0, r)
    terms = np.where(
        r < r_min,
        (r_min - r) ** 2,
        np.where(r > r_max, (r - r_max) ** 2, 0.1 * (r - r_eq) ** 2),
    )
    return float(k_bond * np.sum(terms))

docs.title: e_tot(positions, z_list, *, fast_local_theta=False)
docs.text:
Total folding energy: E_tot = Σ ħc/Θ_i + λ_damp * U_φ.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_tot(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    *,
    fast_local_theta: bool = False,
) -> float:
    """
    Total folding energy: E_tot = Σ ħc/Θ_i + λ_damp * U_φ.
    """
    e_info = e_tot_informational(positions, z_list, fast_local_theta=fast_local_theta)
    e_damp = e_tot_damping(positions, z_list, fast_local_theta=fast_local_theta)
    lambda_damp = 0.1 * HBAR_C_EV_ANG
    return e_info + lambda_damp * e_damp

def e_tot_backbone_with_bonds(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    n_res: int,
    *,
    fast_local_theta: bool = False,
    r_bond_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,

    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
    r_clash: float = R_CLASH,
    k_clash: float = K_CLASH,
    include_clash: bool = True,
) -> float:
    """
    ``e_tot`` + backbone covalent penalties + all-atom clash for non-bonded pairs.

    ``positions`` must be (4 * n_res, 3) in order (N, CA, C, O) per residue.
    """
    e = float(e_tot(positions, z_list, fast_local_theta=fast_local_theta))
    e += e_backbone_bond_penalty(

def e_tot_ca_with_bonds(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    r_eq: float = R_CA_CA_EQ,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    r_clash: float = R_CLASH,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
    k_clash: float = K_CLASH,
    *,
    fast_local_theta: bool = False,
    collective_kink_weight: float = 0.0,
    collective_kink_m: int = 3,
    collective_kink_theta_ref_rad: Optional[float] = None,
    collective_kink_ss_mask: Optional[Any] = None,
    variational_pair_weight: float = 0.0,
    variational_pair_epsilon: float = 0.1,
    variational_pair_sigma: float = 4.0,
    variational_pair_dist_cutoff: float = 12.0,
    variational_pair_min_seq_sep: int = 3,
    variational_pair_max_pairs: int = 400,
    variational_pair_return_bound: bool = False,
    variational_torsion_weight: float = 0.0,
    inertial_twist_weight: float = 0.0,
    inertial_twist_theta_ref_rad: Optional[float] = None,
    inertial_twist_exponent: float = 1.0,
) -> float:
    """
    E_tot + bond-length penalty (consecutive Cα) + clash penalty (non-bonded pairs).
    First principles: atoms "close enough to bond" when r in [r_min, r_max];
    below r_clash for non-bonded = clash. Keeps chain from exploding.

    fast_local_theta: Faster Σ ħc/Θ_i + damping (batched NN distances; numerically same Θ_i).

    collective_kink_weight: If > 0, add Lean-aligned Cα kink budget (``HQIVCollectiveModes``):
        weight × Σ ``K_multipole(m) * helixKinkMeasure(|θᵢ − θ_ref|)`` over interior sites
        (optionally SS-masked). Default ``θ_ref`` from ``default_collective_kink_theta_ref_rad``.

    variational_pair_weight: If > 0, add bounded literature-derived nonlocal pair term:
        ``weight × Σ pair(i,j)`` where each pair contribution is lower-clipped at ``-epsilon``.
        This mirrors Lean ``foldEnergyWithVariationalScore`` lower-bound assumptions.
    variational_pair_return_bound: If True, return tuple ``(energy, lower_bound)`` where lower_bound
        is ``base_fold_energy + variational_pair_lower_bound_term(...)`` on active pairs.
    variational_torsion_weight: Optional nonnegative torsion weight for lower-bound bookkeeping.
    inertial_twist_weight: Weight of center-heavy local bend penalty.
    inertial_twist_theta_ref_rad: Reference local bend angle in radians (default: straight/pi).
    inertial_twist_exponent: Controls center-vs-end weighting sharpness.
    """
    e = e_tot(positions, z_list, fast_local_theta=fast_local_theta)
    n = positions.shape[0]
    e += e_sequential_bond_penalty_ca(
        positions, r_eq=r_eq, r_min=r_min, r_max=r_max, k_bond=k_bond
    )
    window = min(20, n)
    e += e_clash_penalty_ca_windowed(
        positions, r_clash=r_clash, k_clash=k_clash, window=window
    )
    ck_w = float(collective_kink_weight)
    if ck_w != 0.0 and n >= 3:
        from .collective_modes_scalars import (
            default_collective_kink_theta_ref_rad,
            e_ca_collective_kink_sum,
        )

        tr = collective_kink_theta_ref_rad
        if tr is None:
            tr = default_collective_kink_theta_ref_rad()
        e += ck_w * e_ca_collective_kink_sum(
            positions,
            int(collective_kink_m),
            theta_ref_rad=float(tr),
            ss_mask=collective_kink_ss_mask,
        )
    rp_w = float(variational_pair_weight)
    lb = None
    if rp_w != 0.0 and n >= 2:
        n_pairs = count_variational_pairs_ca(
            positions,
            dist_cutoff=float(variational_pair_dist_cutoff),
            min_seq_sep=int(variational_pair_min_seq_sep),
            max_pairs=int(variational_pair_max_pairs),
        )
        lb = e + variational_pair_lower_bound_term(
            n_pairs,
            pair_weight=rp_w,
            epsilon=float(variational_pair_epsilon),
            torsion_weight=float(variational_torsion_weight),
        )
        e += rp_w * e_variational_pair_sum_ca(
            positions,
            epsilon=float(variational_pair_epsilon),
            sigma=float(variational_pair_sigma),
            dist_cutoff=float(variational_pair_dist_cutoff),
            min_seq_sep=int(variational_pair_min_seq_sep),
            max_pairs=int(variational_pair_max_pairs),
        )
    itw = float(inertial_twist_weight)
    if itw != 0.0 and n >= 3:
        e += itw * e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca(
            positions,
            theta_ref_rad=inertial_twist_theta_ref_rad,
            exponent=float(inertial_twist_exponent),
        )
    if variational_pair_return_bound:
        if lb is None:
            lb = float(e)
        return float(e), float(lb)
    return e

docs.title: e_tot_damping(positions, z_list, a_loc=A_LOC_ANG, *, fast_local_theta=False)
docs.text:
Contribution from f_φ: potential energy associated with φ gradient. U_φ ∝ ∫ f_φ·dr ~ Σ_i φ_i / (a_loc + φ_i/6) over neighbors.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_tot_damping(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    a_loc: float = A_LOC_ANG,
    *,
    fast_local_theta: bool = False,
) -> float:
    """
    Contribution from f_φ: potential energy associated with φ gradient.
    U_φ ∝ ∫ f_φ·dr ~ Σ_i φ_i / (a_loc + φ_i/6) over neighbors.
    """
    n = positions.shape[0]
    r_nn = _per_atom_nearest_neighbor_distance(positions) if fast_local_theta and n > 1 else None
    u = 0.0
    for i in range(n):
        if r_nn is not None:
            theta_i = theta_from_nn_distance(r_nn[i], int(z_list[i]))
        else:
            theta_i = theta_at_position(positions, i, int(z_list[i]))
        phi_i = horizon_scalar(theta_i)
        denom = a_loc + phi_i / 6.0
        if denom > 0:
            u += phi_i / denom
    return u

def e_tot_informational(

    z_list: np.ndarray,

) -> float:
    """
    Σ ħ c / Θ_i for all atoms. positions (n, 3) Å, z_list (n) Z_shell.

    fast_local_theta: Use batched nearest-neighbor distances (same Θ_i values, faster for long chains).
    """
    n = positions.shape[0]

    for i in range(n):

docs.title: e_variational_pair_sum_ca(positions, *, epsilon=0.1, sigma=4.0, dist_cutoff=12.0, min_seq_sep=3, max_pairs=400)
docs.text:
Sum bounded literature-aligned pair term over nonlocal Cα pairs.

This is the Python proxy for Lean weighted pair in foldEnergyWithVariationalScore. Each included pair is bounded below by -epsilon.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def e_variational_pair_sum_ca(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    epsilon: float = 0.1,
    sigma: float = 4.0,
    dist_cutoff: float = 12.0,
    min_seq_sep: int = 3,
    max_pairs: int = 400,
) -> float:
    """
    Sum bounded literature-aligned pair term over nonlocal Cα pairs.

    This is the Python proxy for Lean ``weighted pair`` in ``foldEnergyWithVariationalScore``.
    Each included pair is bounded below by ``-epsilon``.
    """
    n = int(positions.shape[0])
    if n < 2:
        return 0.0
    total = 0.0
    count = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + max(1, int(min_seq_sep)), n):
            if count >= int(max_pairs):
                return float(total)
            d = positions[j] - positions[i]
            r = float(np.linalg.norm(d))
            if r < 1e-9 or r > float(dist_cutoff):
                continue
            total += lj_pair_potential_bounded(
                r, epsilon=float(epsilon), sigma=float(sigma)
            )
            count += 1
    return float(total)

docs.title: grad_backbone_bonds_only(positions, n_res, *, r_min=R_BOND_MIN, r_max=R_BOND_MAX, k_bond=K_BOND)
docs.text:
Analytical gradient of backbone bond penalty (N–CA–C–O + peptide C–N).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def grad_backbone_bonds_only(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    n_res: int,
    *,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """Analytical gradient of backbone bond penalty (N–CA–C–O + peptide C–N)."""
    pos = np.asarray(positions, dtype=float)
    n_atom = int(pos.shape[0])
    edges = backbone_bond_edges(n_res)
    grad = np.zeros_like(pos)
    for i, j, r_eq in edges:
        if i >= n_atom or j >= n_atom:
            continue
        d = pos[j] - pos[i]
        r = float(np.linalg.norm(d))
        if r < 1e-12:
            continue
        u = d / r
        g = _bond_stretch_force_scalar(r, float(r_eq), float(r_min), float(r_max), float(k_bond))
        vec = g * u
        grad[i] -= vec
        grad[j] += vec
    return grad

def grad_bonds_only(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    r_eq: float = R_CA_CA_EQ,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    r_clash: float = R_CLASH,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
    k_clash: float = K_CLASH,
    include_clash: bool = False,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Analytical gradient of bond penalty (and optionally clash). O(n) for bonds.
    Used for fast minimization of long chains without finite differences.
    """
    n = positions.shape[0]
    poles = build_bond_poles(positions, r_eq=r_eq, r_min=r_min, r_max=r_max, k_bond=k_bond)
    grad = grad_from_poles(poles, n)
    if include_clash:
        window = min(20, n)
        for i, j, r, u in _clash_pairs(positions, r_clash, window):
            g = -2 * k_clash * (r_clash - r)
            grad[i] -= g * u
            grad[j] += g * u
    return grad

def grad_from_poles(poles: List[Tuple[int, int, np.ndarray]], n: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Accumulate gradient from stored pole vectors: −vec at i, +vec at j."""
    grad = np.zeros((n, 3))
    for i, j, vec in poles:
        grad[i] -= vec
        grad[j] += vec
    return grad

def grad_full(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    include_bonds: bool = True,
    include_horizon: bool = True,
    **kwargs,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Combined gradient: bonds (i,i+1) + full vector sum from all j within horizon.
    Use in fast path for long chains so long-range crowding accumulates.

    Optional HQIV long-range H-bond proxy (Lean ``HQIVLongRange``), additive on Cα:

    - ``hbond_weight`` (default 0): if > 0, add finite-difference gradient of
      ``total_h_bond_proxy_energy_ca`` (``hbond_shell_m``, ``hbond_min_seq_sep``, …).

    Optional collective kink budget (Lean ``HQIVCollectiveModes``): ``collective_kink_weight``,
    ``collective_kink_m``, ``collective_kink_theta_ref_rad``, ``collective_kink_ss_mask``.

    Optional variational pair proxy (Lean ``VariationalScoreTerms``): ``variational_pair_weight`` and
    pair-shape controls. Uses finite-difference gradient (expensive; keep weight 0 for fast path).
    Optional inertia-weighted twist term: ``inertial_twist_weight``.
    """
    grad = np.zeros_like(positions)
    if include_bonds:
        grad += grad_bonds_only(positions, **{k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k in ("r_eq", "r_min", "r_max", "r_clash", "k_bond", "k_clash", "include_clash")})
    if include_horizon:
        grad += grad_horizon_full(
            positions,
            z_list,
            **{k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k in ("r_ref", "r_horizon", "k_horizon", "em_scale", "use_neighbor_list", "neighbor_cutoff")},
        )
    hb_w = float(kwargs.get("hbond_weight", 0.0))
    if hb_w != 0.0:
        from .hqiv_long_range import grad_h_bond_proxy_ca_fd

        m_shell = int(kwargs.get("hbond_shell_m", 3))
        grad = grad + hb_w * grad_h_bond_proxy_ca_fd(
            positions,
            m_shell,
            min_seq_sep=int(kwargs.get("hbond_min_seq_sep", 3)),
            max_pairs=int(kwargs.get("hbond_max_pairs", 200)),
            dist_cutoff=float(kwargs.get("hbond_dist_cutoff", 15.0)),
        )
    ck_w = float(kwargs.get("collective_kink_weight", 0.0))
    if ck_w != 0.0 and positions.shape[0] >= 3:
        from .collective_modes_scalars import (
            default_collective_kink_theta_ref_rad,
            grad_e_ca_collective_kink_fd,
        )

        m_ck = int(kwargs.get("collective_kink_m", 3))
        tr = kwargs.get("collective_kink_theta_ref_rad")
        if tr is None:
            tr = default_collective_kink_theta_ref_rad()
        else:
            tr = float(tr)
        mask = kwargs.get("collective_kink_ss_mask")
        grad = grad + ck_w * grad_e_ca_collective_kink_fd(
            positions,
            m_ck,
            theta_ref_rad=tr,
            ss_mask=mask,
        )
    rp_w = float(kwargs.get("variational_pair_weight", 0.0))
    if rp_w != 0.0 and positions.shape[0] >= 2:
        grad = grad + rp_w * grad_variational_pair_ca_fd(
            positions,
            epsilon=float(kwargs.get("variational_pair_epsilon", 0.1)),
            sigma=float(kwargs.get("variational_pair_sigma", 4.0)),
            dist_cutoff=float(kwargs.get("variational_pair_dist_cutoff", 12.0)),
            min_seq_sep=int(kwargs.get("variational_pair_min_seq_sep", 3)),
            max_pairs=int(kwargs.get("variational_pair_max_pairs", 400)),
        )
    itw = float(kwargs.get("inertial_twist_weight", 0.0))
    if itw != 0.0 and positions.shape[0] >= 3:
        grad = grad + itw * grad_inertial_twist_penalty_ca_fd(
            positions,
            theta_ref_rad=kwargs.get("inertial_twist_theta_ref_rad"),
            exponent=float(kwargs.get("inertial_twist_exponent", 1.0)),
        )
    return grad

def grad_full_backbone(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    n_res: int,
    *,
    include_bonds: bool = True,
    include_horizon: bool = True,
    include_clash: bool = True,
    r_bond_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_bond_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    k_bond: float = K_BOND,
    r_clash: float = R_CLASH,
    k_clash: float = K_CLASH,
    **kwargs: Any,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    HQIV gradient on full heavy-atom backbone: optional covalent bonds + horizon + clash.

    Does not include Cα-only extras from ``grad_full`` (H-bond proxy FD, collective kink, …).
    """
    pos = np.asarray(positions, dtype=float)
    grad = np.zeros_like(pos)
    if include_bonds:
        grad += grad_backbone_bonds_only(
            pos, n_res, r_min=r_bond_min, r_max=r_bond_max, k_bond=k_bond
        )
    if include_horizon:
        grad += grad_horizon_full(
            pos,
            z_list,
            **{k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k in ("r_ref", "r_horizon", "k_horizon", "em_scale", "use_neighbor_list", "neighbor_cutoff")},
        )
    if include_clash:
        bonded = bonded_set_from_edges(backbone_bond_edges(n_res))
        grad += grad_clash_nonbonded(pos, bonded, r_clash=r_clash, k_clash=k_clash)
    return grad

    em_scale: float = 1.0,
    neighbor_cutoff: Optional[float] = None,
):
    """
    Full vector sum of horizon forces: every atom j contributes to i.
    F_i += pot(r_ij) * unit_vector(i→j) with pot from Θ_ij, φ.
    Repulsive crowding: grad[i] -= pot * unit (push i away from close j).
    With neighbor list (default): O(n·k) for k neighbors within cutoff; 3–8× faster on long chains.
    If return_poles=True, returns (grad, poles) where poles is a list of (i, j, vec) bond potential
    vectors (vec points i→j; − at i, + at j).

    em_scale: Same as in build_horizon_poles (e.g. 1/ε_r in water).
    neighbor_cutoff: Optional tighter neighbor-list radius (Å); see ``build_horizon_poles``.
    """
    poles = build_horizon_poles(
        positions, z_list, r_ref=r_ref, r_horizon=r_horizon,
        k_horizon=k_horizon, use_neighbor_list=use_neighbor_list, em_scale=em_scale,
        neighbor_cutoff=neighbor_cutoff,
    )
    n = positions.shape[0]
    grad = grad_from_poles(poles, n)
    if return_poles:
        return grad, poles
    return grad

docs.text:
Finite-difference gradient of e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def grad_inertial_twist_penalty_ca_fd(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    theta_ref_rad: Optional[float] = None,
    exponent: float = 1.0,
    eps: float = 1e-3,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """Finite-difference gradient of ``e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca``."""
    pos = np.asarray(positions, dtype=float)
    n = pos.shape[0]
    g = np.zeros_like(pos)
    for i in range(n):
        for d in range(3):
            pp = pos.copy()
            pm = pos.copy()
            pp[i, d] += eps
            pm[i, d] -= eps
            ep = e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca(pp, theta_ref_rad=theta_ref_rad, exponent=exponent)
            em = e_inertial_twist_penalty_ca(pm, theta_ref_rad=theta_ref_rad, exponent=exponent)
            g[i, d] = (ep - em) / (2.0 * eps)
    return g

Gradient of Rg² w.r.t. positions: (2/n)(r_i - r_com). Pulls atoms toward COM.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def grad_rg_squared(positions: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Gradient of Rg² w.r.t. positions: (2/n)(r_i - r_com). Pulls atoms toward COM."""
    n = positions.shape[0]
    if n < 2:
        return np.zeros_like(positions)
    com = np.mean(positions, axis=0)
    return (2.0 / n) * (positions - com)

def grad_variational_pair_ca_fd(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    *,
    epsilon: float = 0.1,
    sigma: float = 4.0,
    dist_cutoff: float = 12.0,
    min_seq_sep: int = 3,
    max_pairs: int = 400,
    eps: float = 1e-3,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """Finite-difference gradient of ``e_variational_pair_sum_ca`` w.r.t. Cα positions."""
    pos = np.asarray(positions, dtype=float)
    n = pos.shape[0]
    g = np.zeros_like(pos)
    for i in range(n):
        for d in range(3):
            pos_p = pos.copy()
            pos_p[i, d] += eps
            pos_m = pos.copy()
            pos_m[i, d] -= eps
            ep = e_variational_pair_sum_ca(
                pos_p,
                epsilon=epsilon,
                sigma=sigma,
                dist_cutoff=dist_cutoff,

                dist_cutoff=dist_cutoff,

                max_pairs=max_pairs,

docs.title: lj_pair_potential_bounded(r, *, epsilon=0.1, sigma=4.0)
docs.text:
Literature-aligned bounded Lennard-Jones pair proxy with lower bound >= -epsilon.

Uses a clipped Lennard-Jones-style scalar

4 ε [ (σ/r)^12 - (σ/r)^6 ], then lower-clipped at -ε.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def lj_pair_potential_bounded(
    r: float,
    *,
    epsilon: float = 0.1,
    sigma: float = 4.0,
) -> float:
    """
    Literature-aligned bounded Lennard-Jones pair proxy with lower bound ``>= -epsilon``.

    Uses a clipped Lennard-Jones-style scalar:
      4 ε [ (σ/r)^12 - (σ/r)^6 ], then lower-clipped at ``-ε``.
    """
    rr = max(float(r), 1e-9)
    eps = max(float(epsilon), 0.0)
    sig = max(float(sigma), 1e-9)
    x = sig / rr
    val = 4.0 * eps * (x**12 - x**6)
    return float(max(-eps, val))

docs.title: minimize_e_tot(positions_init, z_list, steps=200, step_size=0.01)
docs.text:
Deterministic gradient descent on E_tot (no random seed). positions_init (n, 3) Å, z_list (n). Returns (positions_opt, {"e_final": ..., "e_initial": ...}). For L-BFGS (recommended), use gradient_descent_folding.minimize_e_tot_lbfgs.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def minimize_e_tot(
    positions_init: np.ndarray,
    z_list: np.ndarray,
    steps: int = 200,
    step_size: float = 0.01,
) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, Dict[str, float]]:
    """
    Deterministic gradient descent on E_tot (no random seed). positions_init (n, 3) Å, z_list (n).
    Returns (positions_opt, {"e_final": ..., "e_initial": ...}).
    For L-BFGS (recommended), use gradient_descent_folding.minimize_e_tot_lbfgs.
    """
    pos = np.array(positions_init, dtype=float)
    e0 = e_tot(pos, z_list)
    n = pos.shape[0]
    for _ in range(steps):
        grad = np.zeros_like(pos)
        for j in range(n):
            for d in range(3):
                pos[j, d] += 1e-5
                e_plus = e_tot(pos, z_list)
                pos[j, d] -= 2e-5
                e_minus = e_tot(pos, z_list)
                pos[j, d] += 1e-5
                grad[j, d] = (e_plus - e_minus) / (2e-5)
        pos -= step_size * grad
    e_final = e_tot(pos, z_list)
    return pos, {"e_final": e_final, "e_initial": e0}

docs.title: project_backbone_covalent_bonds(positions, n_res, *, r_min=R_BOND_MIN, r_max=R_BOND_MAX, passes=3)
docs.text:
Project each backbone covalent edge toward [r_min, r_max] (half-step on each end).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def project_backbone_covalent_bonds(
    positions: np.ndarray,
    n_res: int,
    *,
    r_min: float = R_BOND_MIN,
    r_max: float = R_BOND_MAX,
    passes: int = 3,
) -> np.ndarray:
    """Project each backbone covalent edge toward [r_min, r_max] (half-step on each end)."""
    pos = np.asarray(positions, dtype=float).copy()
    edges = backbone_bond_edges(n_res)
    for _ in range(max(1, int(passes))):
        for i, j, _ in edges:
            d = pos[j] - pos[i]
            r = float(np.linalg.norm(d))
            if r < 1e-12:
                continue
            u = d / r
            if r < float(r_min):
                tgt = float(r_min)
            elif r > float(r_max):
                tgt = float(r_max)
            else:
                continue
            delta = 0.5 * (r - tgt) * u
            pos[i] += delta
            pos[j] -= delta
    return pos

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def rg_squared(positions: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Rg² = (1/n) Σ_i |r_i - r_com|². Used for collapse bias (minimize to compactify)."""
    n = positions.shape[0]
    if n < 2:
        return 0.0
    com = np.mean(positions, axis=0)
    return float(np.mean(np.sum((positions - com) ** 2, axis=1)))

def small_peptide_energy(sequence: str) -> Dict[str, float]:
    """
    E_tot for a small peptide: backbone-only Cα trace, Z=6 for Cα.
    sequence: one-letter amino acids. Returns {"e_tot": ..., "per_residue": ...}.
    """
    n = len(sequence)
    # Simple linear chain spacing 3.8 Å (extended)
    positions = np.zeros((n, 3))
    positions[:, 0] = np.arange(n) * 3.8
    z_list = np.full(n, 6)
    e = e_tot(positions, z_list)
    return {"e_tot": e, "per_residue": e / n if n else 0.0}

docs.title: theta_at_position(positions, i, z_shell, coordination=2)
docs.text:
Θ at node i from position (diamond size from nearest-neighbor distances). Θ_local = base Θ scaled by local density: higher density → smaller Θ (monogamy).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def theta_at_position(positions: np.ndarray, i: int, z_shell: int, coordination: int = 2) -> float:
    """
    Θ at node i from position (diamond size from nearest-neighbor distances).
    Θ_local = base Θ scaled by local density: higher density → smaller Θ (monogamy).
    """
    base = theta_local(z_shell, coordination)
    if positions.shape[0] < 2:
        return base
    d = np.linalg.norm(positions - positions[i], axis=1)
    d[i] = np.inf
    r_min = np.min(d)
    return theta_from_nn_distance(r_min, z_shell, coordination)

docs.title: theta_from_nn_distance(r_nn, z_shell, coordination=2)
docs.text:
Θ_i from nearest-neighbor distance r_nn (same crowding rule as theta_at_position).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def theta_from_nn_distance(r_nn: float, z_shell: int, coordination: int = 2) -> float:
    """
    Θ_i from nearest-neighbor distance r_nn (same crowding rule as ``theta_at_position``).
    """
    base = theta_local(z_shell, coordination)
    r_ref = 2.0
    if not np.isfinite(r_nn) or r_nn >= 1e30:
        return base
    return base * min(1.0, float(r_nn) / r_ref)

docs.title: variational_pair_lower_bound_term(n_pairs, *, pair_weight, epsilon, torsion_weight=0.0)
docs.text:
Lean-aligned lower-bound term for additive variational score pieces.

If pair term is bounded below by -epsilon per active pair and torsion term is nonnegative, then: w_pair * pair + w_torsion * torsion >= w_pair * (-epsilon * n_pairs) + w_torsion * 0 for nonnegative weights.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/folding_energy.py 
def variational_pair_lower_bound_term(
    n_pairs: int,
    *,
    pair_weight: float,
    epsilon: float,
    torsion_weight: float = 0.0,
) -> float:
    """
    Lean-aligned lower-bound term for additive variational score pieces.

    If pair term is bounded below by ``-epsilon`` per active pair and torsion term is
    nonnegative, then:
      ``w_pair * pair + w_torsion * torsion >= w_pair * (-epsilon * n_pairs) + w_torsion * 0``
    for nonnegative weights.
    """
    w_pair = max(0.0, float(pair_weight))
    w_tor = max(0.0, float(torsion_weight))
    eps = max(0.0, float(epsilon))
    n = max(0, int(n_pairs))
    return (-w_pair * eps * float(n)) + (w_tor * 0.0)

After every step, project so bonds remain valid; clash penalty for non-bonded < 2.0 Å. Then reconstruct full backbone. No force fields; all from first principles.

docs.text:
Format minimizer result as PDB string (REMARK HQIV-QConSi-*-step + MODEL ... ENDMDL END). Protein ATOM then ligand HETATM if provided.

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/full_protein_minimizer.py 
def full_chain_to_pdb(
    result: Dict[str, object],
    chain_id: str = "A",
    ligands: Optional[List[LigandAgent]] = None,
    ligand_chain_id: Optional[str] = None,
    *,
    model_step: Optional[int] = None,
) -> str:
    """Format minimizer result as PDB string (REMARK HQIV-QConSi-*-step + MODEL ... ENDMDL END). Protein ATOM then ligand HETATM if provided."""
    backbone_atoms = result["backbone_atoms"]
    sequence = result["sequence"]
    include_sidechains = result.get("include_sidechains", False)
    if ligands is None:
        ligands = result.get("ligands") or []
    if not backbone_atoms and not ligands:
        return hqiv_qconsi_empty_pdb_block(step=model_step)
    het_chain = ligand_chain_id if ligand_chain_id is not None else chain_id
    lines = list(hqiv_qconsi_model_lines(step=model_step))
    atom_id = 1
    n_res = result["n_res"]
    idx = 0
    for res_id in range(1, n_res + 1):
        res_1 = sequence[res_id - 1]
        res_3 = AA_1to3.get(res_1, "UNK")
        n_atoms_this = (5 if res_1 != "G" else 4) if include_sidechains else 4
        for _ in range(n_atoms_this):
            name, xyz = backbone_atoms[idx]
            lines.append(
                f"ATOM  {atom_id:5d}  {name:2s}  {res_3:3s} {chain_id}{res_id:4d}    "
                f"{float(xyz[0]):8.3f}{float(xyz[1]):8.3f}{float(xyz[2]):8.3f}  1.00  0.00           "
            )
            atom_id += 1
            idx += 1
    def _pdb_coord(x: float) -> str:
        s = f"{float(x):8.3f}"
        return s[-8:] if len(s) > 8 else s

    for res_id_het, lig in enumerate(ligands, start=1):
        res_3 = (lig.res_name or "LIG")[:3]
        for a, (name, xyz) in enumerate(zip(lig.atom_names, lig.get_world_positions())):
            elem = (lig.elements[a] if a < len(lig.elements) else "C")[:2].rjust(2)
            line = (
                f"HETATM{atom_id:5d}  {name:2s}  {res_3:3s} {het_chain}{res_id_het:4d}    "
                f"{_pdb_coord(xyz[0])}{_pdb_coord(xyz[1])}{_pdb_coord(xyz[2])}  1.00  0.00      {elem:>2s}"
            )
            lines.append(line[:80] if len(line) > 80 else line)
            atom_id += 1
    lines.append("ENDMDL")
    lines.append("END")
    return "\n".join(lines)

docs.title: merged_multichain_backbone_atoms(result_and_chain_ids, *, chain_gap=50.0)
docs.text:
Concatenate backbone (N,CA,C,O,…) from several minimizer result dicts with +x offsets, matching the layout used by casp_server._merge_pdb_chains (chain–chain separation).

Source code in src/horizon_physics/proteins/full_protein_minimizer.py 
def merged_multichain_backbone_atoms(
    result_and_chain_ids: List[Tuple[Dict[str, object], str]],
    *,
    chain_gap: float = 50.0,
) -> List[Tuple[str, np.ndarray]]:
    """
    Concatenate backbone (N,CA,C,O,…) from several minimizer ``result`` dicts with +x offsets,
    matching the layout used by ``casp_server._merge_pdb_chains`` (chain–chain separation).
    """
    chain_mins: List[float] = []
    for result, _ in result_and_chain_ids:
        bb = result.get("backbone_atoms") or []
        if not bb:
            chain_mins.append(0.0)
        else:
            chain_mins.append(min(float(xyz[0]) for _, xyz in bb))
    out: List[Tuple[str, np.ndarray]] = []
    prev_max_x = float("-inf")
    for ci, (result, _) in enumerate(result_and_chain_ids):
        backbone_atoms = result.get("backbone_atoms") or []
        sequence = str(result.get("sequence") or "")
        include_sidechains = bool(result.get("include_sidechains", False))
        n_res = int(result.get("n_res") or 0)
        if not backbone_atoms or not sequence or n_res <= 0:
            continue
        offset_x = max(0.0, prev_max_x + chain_gap - chain_mins[ci])
        idx = 0
        chain_max_x = float("-inf")
        for res_id in range(1, n_res + 1):
            res_1 = sequence[res_id - 1]
            n_atoms_this = (5 if res_1 != "G" else 4) if include_sidechains else 4
            for _ in range(n_atoms_this):
                name, xyz = backbone_atoms[idx]
                x = float(xyz[0]) + offset_x
                y, z = float(xyz[1]), float(xyz[2])
                chain_max_x = max(chain_max_x, x)
                out.append((name, np.asarray([x, y, z], dtype=np.float64)))
                idx += 1
        prev_max_x = chain_max_x
    return out

Full-chain minimization: minimize E_tot over Cα, then rebuild backbone.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default sequence str

One-letter amino acid sequence (or will be parsed from FASTA if needed).

required ca_init Optional[ndarray]

Initial Cα positions (n_res, 3) in Å. If None, from SS-aware placement.

None ss_string Optional[str]

Optional SS string (H/E/C). If None and ca_init is None, predict_ss(sequence).

None max_iter int

L-BFGS max iterations (short chains).

500 gtol float

Gradient tolerance for convergence.

1e-05 include_sidechains bool

If True, add Cβ positions (from ideal geometry); no full side chains yet.

False include_ligands bool

If True and ligands is non-empty, run 6-DOF rigid-body refinement for ligands against the fixed protein (horizon + clash). Default False for pure CAMEO compatibility.

False ligands Optional[List[LigandAgent]]

List of LigandAgent (from parse_ligands). Used only when include_ligands=True.

None fast_horizon bool

If True, use bonds-only gradient (nearest-neighbor); faster, for debugging.

False side_chain_pack bool

If True and include_sidechains, run lightweight Cβ rotamer search after backbone.

True quick bool

If True, rapid prototyping: no side chains, fewer iterations, relaxed gtol, bonds-only for long chains.

False collapse bool

If True and n_res > 50, use two-stage annealing (Rg collapse then refine) for globule formation.

True long_chain_max_iter Optional[int]

Max iterations for long-chain path (default 250). Used when n_res > 50.

None collapse_init_steps Optional[int]

Optional Rg-only steps before main loop to get compact init (default 40 when collapse=True).

None k_rg_collapse Optional[float]

Weight for Rg² term during collapse (default K_RG_COLLAPSE). Increase for stronger pull toward compact.

None trajectory_log_path Optional[str]

If set, write each step's Cα positions to this file as JSONL (one {"t": step, "positions": [...]} per line, flush each line) for Manim or other viewers; use with tail -f to watch the run.

None signal_dump_path Optional[str]

If set, on SIGINT/SIGTERM (e.g. Ctrl+C) write the current Cα state to this path as a PDB and exit. Lets you recover a partial run.

None simulate_ribosome_tunnel bool

If True, use co-translational mode: null search cone (exit tunnel), plane at lip to null unphysical re-entry, fast-pass spaghetti (rigid group + bell-end large translations), and a short HKE min pass on each connection event. Default False for backward compatibility.

False tunnel_length float

Length of tunnel in Å along extrusion axis (default 25.0). Residues with Cα inside this segment are cone-constrained.

25.0 cone_half_angle_deg float

Half-angle of the cone in degrees (default 12.0). Cα inside the tunnel must stay within this cone from the peptidyl transferase center.

12.0 lip_plane_distance float

Extra distance in Å beyond tunnel_length for the lip plane (default 0.0). Lip is at tunnel_length + lip_plane_distance along the axis.

0.0 post_extrusion_refine bool

When simulate_ribosome_tunnel=True, if True (default), run post-extrusion refinement after the chain leaves the cone/plane (see post_extrusion_refine_mode).

True post_extrusion_refine_mode str

"em_treetorque" (default) = 3D EM-field relax_to_convergence with alternate_hke=False (no grad_full Cα step), then discrete run_discrete_refinement (tree-torque). "hke" = legacy repeated _minimize_bonds_fast / L-BFGS rounds. "none" = skip.

'em_treetorque' post_extrusion_em_max_steps Optional[int]

Cap on EM relax steps (None = length-scaled inside refine_ca_post_tunnel_em_treetorque).

None post_extrusion_treetorque_phases int

Max phases for run_discrete_refinement (when mode is em_treetorque).

8 post_extrusion_treetorque_n_steps int

Inner discrete steps per phase cap (Metropolis loop length).

200 refinement_temperature_k float

Kelvin-like scale for run_discrete_refinement and thermal Cα relax when post mode is em_treetorque.

310.0 post_extrusion_discrete_metropolis bool

If True, discrete φ/ψ moves use Metropolis at refinement_temperature_k (else greedy downhill).

False post_extrusion_discrete_seed Optional[int]

RNG seed for Metropolis / thermal relax (None = non-deterministic stream).

None post_extrusion_anneal bool

If True and post mode is em_treetorque, run a short multi-stage Metropolis cool-down instead of one discrete pass (see post_extrusion_anneal_schedule_k).

False post_extrusion_anneal_schedule_k Optional[Tuple[float, ...]]

Optional Kelvin schedule (≥2 values, high→low); last stage should match refinement_temperature_k for consistent Langevin tail.

None post_extrusion_langevin_steps int

After discrete refine, number of thermal_gradient_relax_ca steps (0 = off).

0 post_extrusion_langevin_noise_fraction float

Scales RMS of Gaussian noise vs step_size in thermal relax.

0.2 post_extrusion_max_disp_floor float

(hke mode only) Minimum motion threshold (Å) for stopping post-extrusion iterations.

0.25 post_extrusion_max_rounds int

(hke mode only) Safety cap on outer rounds (each runs one _minimize_bonds_fast collapse+refine).

32 post_extrusion_osh_hqiv_native bool

After EM/tree-torque or HKE post-extrusion (when mode is not none), run minimize_ca_with_osh_oracle with Lean HQIV-native π-phase sparse gates (OSHoracleHQIVNative).

False post_extrusion_osh_n_iter / _step_size / _ansatz_depth / _gate_mix / _hqiv_reference_m

Pass-through to that OSHoracle refine.

required fast_pass_steps_per_connection int

Co-translational rigid-group + bell gradient steps per segment (set 0 to skip).

5 min_pass_iter_per_connection int

L-BFGS iterations per segment in the tunnel (connection-triggered “HKE” pass; set 0 to skip).

15 tunnel_axis Optional[ndarray]

(3,) unit vector for tunnel axis; default +Z. Chain is aligned so N-terminus is at origin and extrusion is along this axis.

None hke_above_tunnel_fraction float

When simulate_ribosome_tunnel=True, HKE (connection-triggered L-BFGS) is applied only to residues above this fraction of the tunnel length (default 0.5 = 50%). The chain is built with the fast method (rigid group + bell-end); only the part above this threshold is updated by the min pass.

0.5 tunnel_thermal_gradient_steps int

When simulate_ribosome_tunnel=True and >0, after each binary-tree segment's masked L-BFGS pass, run this many kT-noised gradient steps with the same cone/plane/HKE-fraction masking as L-BFGS. Uses refinement_temperature_k as the thermal scale. Default 0 = deterministic tunnel (legacy).

0 tunnel_thermal_noise_fraction float

RMS multiplier for Gaussian Cα noise vs step size (same role as post-extrusion Langevin).

0.2 tunnel_thermal_step_size Optional[float]

Cα step scale; None → 0.022 if quick else 0.028.

None tunnel_thermal_reference_temperature_k float

Denominator for √(T/T_ref) noise scaling (typically 310 K).

310.0 tunnel_thermal_seed Optional[int]

RNG seed for tunnel thermal steps; None uses post_extrusion_discrete_seed if set, else non-deterministic.

None em_scale float

Multiplier on horizon (EM) pole strength in grad_full (default 1). Use 1/ε_r for bulk water screening (HQIV Lean waterDielectricValley).

1.0 kappa_dihedral float

Weight on Lean foldEnergyWithDihedral correction κ(1−cos Δθ) on φ/ψ toward the HQIV α Ramachandran basin (default 0 = off).

0.0 hbond_weight float

Weight on Lean HQIVLongRange hBondProxy gradient (finite differences on Cα; default 0 = off). Expensive for long chains.

0.0 hbond_shell_m int

Nuclear/shell index m for K_hbond m, R_hbond m (default 3).

3 hbond_min_seq_sep, hbond_max_pairs, hbond_dist_cutoff

Pair list for total_h_bond_proxy_energy_ca.

required fast_local_theta bool

If True, evaluate Σ ħc/Θ_i + damping in e_tot_ca_with_bonds via batched nearest-neighbor distances (same Θ_i as the legacy loop, faster for long chains).

False horizon_neighbor_cutoff Optional[float]

If set, cap the spatial neighbor list radius (Å) in grad_full horizon forces below module default CUTOFF (prunes weak long-range pairs; faster, approximate).

None collective_kink_weight float

Weight on Lean HQIVCollectiveModes Cα kink budget (0 = off).

0.0 collective_kink_m int

Shell index m for K_multipole.

3 collective_kink_theta_ref_rad Optional[float]

Interior Cα bend reference (rad); default from HQIV α-helix trace.

None collective_kink_use_ss_mask bool

If True, only apply kink budget at residues predicted H (predict_ss).

False variational_pair_weight float

Weight on Lean VariationalScoreTerms bounded nonlocal pair proxy (0 = off). Added to fold score as w_pair * pair_sum.

0.0 variational_pair_epsilon float

Per-pair depth parameter ε for bounded pair proxy. Lower bound per pair is -ε by construction, matching Lean lower-bound assumptions.

0.1 variational_pair_sigma float

LJ-like pair radius scale σ (Å) for bounded pair proxy.

4.0 variational_pair_dist_cutoff float

Pair cutoff (Å) for nonlocal variational pair term.

12.0 variational_pair_min_seq_sep int

Minimum sequence separation |i-j| for pair term.

3 variational_pair_max_pairs int

Safety cap on evaluated pair terms.

400 variational_staged_opt bool

If True and variational pair is enabled, run a staged objective: first optimize with variational pair off, then refine with full variational weight.

False variational_stage_frac float

Fraction of iteration budget used by stage-1 warmup (0..1).

0.5 variational_bound_prune bool

If True, use Lean-style lower-bound gating in long-chain fast refinement to skip expensive variational FD gradients when they cannot beat best seen.

False variational_bound_prune_margin float

Safety margin (energy units) for bound gating.

0.0 inertial_twist_weight float

Weight on center-heavy local bend penalty so central twists are penalized more than terminal twists.

0.0 inertial_twist_theta_ref_rad Optional[float]

Reference local bend angle (rad) for inertial twist term.

None inertial_twist_exponent float

Shape exponent for center-heavy weighting profile.

1.0 ligand_refine_steps, ligand_step_t, ligand_step_ang

Rigid-body refinement of ligands when include_ligands is True. Default ligand_refinement_mode="lean_qc" matches Lean ProteinQCRefinement (soft clash). Use "horizon" for legacy grad_full screening (hbond_weight forced off).

required ligand_refinement_mode str

lean_qc (default) or horizon.

'lean_qc' qc_soft_clash_sigma, qc_clash_weight

Lean QC soft-clash distance scale (Å) and weight (only for lean_qc).

required

Returns:

Type Description Dict[str, object]

dict with: ca_min: (n_res, 3) minimized Cα in Å. backbone_atoms: list of (atom_name, xyz) per residue (N, CA, C, O). E_ca_final: E_tot evaluated on Cα only (eV). E_backbone_final: E_tot evaluated on full backbone N,CA,C,O (eV). info: from minimize_e_tot_lbfgs (e_final, e_initial, n_iter, success, message). n_res: number of residues. sequence: sequence used.
